etymology.description: phonetic 我 (wǒ, "I") + semantic 羊 (yáng, "good; beautiful; auspicious") -> righteousness
etymology.interpretation: The character 義 combines the phonetic component 我 (wǒ, "I") with the semantic component 羊 (yáng, "good; beautiful; auspicious"). The 羊 component signifies qualities like goodness and beauty, indicating righteousness as something inherently valuable or noble. The pronunciation element 我 contributes the sound without semantic value in this context.
etymology.simplification: 义 is a simplified form of 義, where 羊 (yáng, "good") is removed, and 我 (wǒ, "I") is simplified to form a new character emphasizing ease of writing while retaining the general phonetic guide.

examples.chinese: 他为了正义(他為了正義)，勇敢地站出来(勇敢地站出來)。
examples.pinyin: Tā wèile zhèngyì, yǒnggǎn de zhàn chūlái.
examples.english: He stood up bravely for justice.

examples.chinese: 这个故事的意义(這個故事的意義)在于勇气(在於勇氣)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège gùshì de yìyì zàiyú yǒngqì.
examples.english: The meaning of this story lies in courage.

examples.chinese: 义务帮助他人(義務幫助他人)是一种美德(是一種美德)。
examples.pinyin: Yìwù bāngzhù tārén shì yī zhǒng měidé.
examples.english: Voluntary helping others is a virtue.
